kit: cult/ui (Cult UI) — free, open-source animated React components built on shadcn/ui conventions + motion (framer-motion) + Tailwind CSS; ~78 free components, source at github.com/nolly-studio/cult-ui
installation: shadcn registry, per-component. Two documented paths: (1) direct URL — `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://cult-ui.com/r/{slug}.json` (e.g. `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://cult-ui.com/r/minimal-card.json`); (2) registry alias — add `"registries": { "@cult-ui": "https://cult-ui.com/r/{name}.json" }` to components.json, then `npx shadcn@beta add @cult-ui/[component-name]`. Components are distributed as source (copied into your codebase), so manual copy-paste from docs/GitHub also works; shadcn itself is not mandatory but the `cn` util (clsx + tailwind-merge) is expected.
dependencies: motion (framer-motion successor — core animation dep for most components)
lucide-react (icons used in several demos/components, standard shadcn peer)
tailwindVersion: Tailwind CSS v4 (docs specify tailwindcss@latest + @tailwindcss/postcss; no tailwind.config needed, CSS-first config). Components use standard utility classes and also work in v3-era shadcn setups, but the documented install targets v4 — matches your Astro 5 + Tailwind v4 stack.

components.name: Gradient Heading
components.blogUse: Hero title and section headings (h1/h2) — variants default/pink/light, sizes lg–xxxl; 'light'/'default' variants stay restrained on dark backgrounds

components.name: Text Animate
components.blogUse: Hero title/subtitle entrance animation (fadeInUp/calmInUp are subtle enough for Swiss-minimal); needs 'use client' → client:load island

components.name: Minimal Card
components.blogUse: Post card grid on homepage and tags page — MinimalCard + MinimalCardImage/Title/Description map 1:1 to post cover/title/excerpt; literally the minimal aesthetic you want

components.name: Code Block
components.blogUse: Post-page code blocks: tabs, copy button, dark-mode compatible, keyboard nav — hydrate as client:visible island inside MDX prose

components.name: Direction Aware Tabs
components.blogUse: Tags page filter tabs (All / by-tag) with direction-correct slide animation; also usable for code-example tab groups

components.name: Popover Form
components.blogUse: Newsletter CTA: headless popover form with loading + success states and keyboard shortcuts — footer or end-of-post subscribe

components.name: Texture Button
components.blogUse: Site-wide button system (newsletter subscribe, 'read more', 404 back-home) — 'minimal' and 'secondary' variants fit a Swiss dark theme

components.name: Stripe Bg Guides
components.blogUse: Subtle Stripe-style vertical grid guide lines behind hero/homepage — the most Swiss-grid-compatible background in the kit; contained + glowColor props, keep animation off or slow

components.name: Side Panel
components.blogUse: Mobile nav drawer and/or slide-in TOC panel on post pages (renderButton + children API)

components.name: Expandable Card
components.blogUse: Collapsible TOC on mobile, expandable post-card details, FAQ-style sections on about page (generic condense/expand primitive; slug is 'expandable', not 'expandable-card')

components.name: Texture Card
components.blogUse: Newsletter CTA card or about-page bio card (Header/Content/Footer/Separator subcomponents); neumorphic undertone — use sparingly in a flat design

components.name: Neumorph Eyebrow
components.blogUse: Kicker/eyebrow label above post titles or section headings ('Latest posts', tag badges)

components.name: Shift Card
components.blogUse: Featured/pinned post card with hover shift micro-interaction on the homepage

components.name: Cutout Card
components.blogUse: Alternate card treatment for a featured post or about-page highlight

components.name: Animated Number
components.blogUse: About-page stats (posts written, years coding) or animated reading-time counter; spring-configurable, needs client JS

components.name: Typewriter
components.blogUse: Hero subtitle cycling through topics ('writing about X / Y / Z'); slightly gimmicky for strict minimalism

components.name: Squiggle Arrow
components.blogUse: Hand-drawn SVG arrow pointing at the newsletter CTA or 404 'go home' link; direction + wavy/bouncy/smooth variants

components.name: Edge Blur
components.blogUse: Progressive blur fade at top/bottom viewport edges under sticky header / above footer — very fitting for minimal dark

components.name: Texture Overlay
components.blogUse: Subtle grain/noise overlay on the dark page background — classic minimal-dark garnish

components.name: Background Texture
components.blogUse: Alternative full-page subtle texture for body background

components.name: Grid Beam
components.blogUse: Hero background: soft glowing beams along grid dividers ('mono' colorVariant + dark theme keeps it subtle); canvas-based, client-only

components.name: Family Drawer
components.blogUse: Mobile menu / share sheet drawer (Family-app-style multi-view drawer)

components.name: Floating Panel
components.blogUse: Floating TOC or share/actions panel on post pages

components.name: Popover
components.blogUse: Footnote/reference previews and definition popovers inside prose

components.name: Toolbar Expandable
components.blogUse: Floating expandable toolbar: theme switcher + share + scroll-to-top cluster on post pages

components.name: Dynamic Island
components.blogUse: Playful notification/now-playing widget in header; charming but not Swiss-minimal — garnish only

components.name: Family Button
components.blogUse: Expanding action button (share / like cluster) at end of posts

components.name: Intro Disclosure
components.blogUse: First-visit 'what's new on the blog' walkthrough with localStorage don't-show-again; dialog on desktop, drawer on mobile — probably overkill

components.name: Browser Window
components.blogUse: Framing screenshots/demos inside technical posts

components.name: Terminal Animation
components.blogUse: Animated terminal for dev-blog hero or CLI tutorials in posts

components.name: Hover Video Player
components.blogUse: Hover-to-play video embeds in posts or on project cards

components.name: YouTube Video Player
components.blogUse: Lightweight-facade YouTube embeds inside posts

components.name: LightBoard
components.blogUse: Playful LED-marquee '404 NOT FOUND' message on the 404 page — one flashy exception that works there

components.name: Bg Animate Button
components.blogUse: Animated-gradient CTA button; too loud for primary use, possible single newsletter CTA accent

components.name: Hero Static Radial Gradient
components.blogUse: Full hero section with shader radial gradient — visually closest hero to 'minimal dark', but pulls @paper-design/shaders-react (WebGL) — heavy for a blog

components.name: Bg Media Hero
components.blogUse: Hero with background image/video — only if you ever want a photographic hero

components.name: MacOS Dock
components.blogUse: Magnifying dock of social links in the footer; fun but off-brand for Swiss minimal

components.name: Neumorph Button
components.blogUse: Theme-switcher toggle or icon buttons; neumorphism clashes with flat Swiss style — use only if you like the soft look

components.name: Gradient Button Group
components.blogUse: Grouped filter buttons on tags page; gradient styling is louder than needed

components.name: Border Beam Button
components.blogUse: Animated border-beam CTA — too flashy for minimal blog

components.name: Metal Button
components.blogUse: Skeuomorphic metal button — off-style

components.name: Cosmic Button
components.blogUse: Cosmic/glow button — off-style

components.name: Hero Dithering
components.blogUse: Shader dithering hero — heavy WebGL, too flashy

components.name: Hero Color Panels
components.blogUse: Colorful panel hero — clashes with minimal dark palette

components.name: Hero Heatmap
components.blogUse: Heatmap shader hero — heavy/flashy

components.name: Hero Liquid Metal
components.blogUse: Liquid-metal shader hero — heavy/flashy

components.name: Logo Carousel
components.blogUse: Client-logo marquee — marketing pattern, no blog fit

components.name: Tweet Grid
components.blogUse: Grid of embedded tweets — external deps, not needed for a minimal blog

components.name: Distorted Glass
components.blogUse: Glass distortion effect — too flashy

components.name: Dither Image
components.blogUse: Dithered image effect — stylistic mismatch (unless you adopt a retro art direction)

components.name: Fractal Grid
components.blogUse: Fractal grid visual — busy, anti-minimal

components.name: Canvas Fractal Grid
components.blogUse: Canvas fractal background — heavy and busy

components.name: Shader Lens Blur
components.blogUse: WebGL lens blur — heavy shader dep

components.name: SVG Shapes
components.blogUse: Decorative SVG shape packs — no blog role

components.name: SVG Shapes Animated
components.blogUse: Animated decorative shapes — no blog role

components.name: SVG Bands
components.blogUse: Decorative SVG bands — no blog role

components.name: Onboarding
components.blogUse: App onboarding flow — blogs don't onboard

components.name: Expandable Screen
components.blogUse: Full-screen expansion widget — app pattern, not blog

components.name: Morph Surface
components.blogUse: Morphing surface widget — app pattern

components.name: Color Picker
components.blogUse: Color input — no blog role

components.name: Choice Poll
components.blogUse: Poll widget — needs backend, out of scope

components.name: Feature Poll
components.blogUse: Feature poll — product pattern

components.name: Feature Voting
components.blogUse: Feature voting — product pattern

components.name: Vote Tally
components.blogUse: Vote tally display — product pattern

components.name: Poll Widget
components.blogUse: Embedded poll in a post could be fun, but needs a backend — skip for now

components.name: Prompt Library
components.blogUse: AI prompt library UI — unrelated

components.name: AI Instructions
components.blogUse: AI instruction editor — unrelated

components.name: Timer
components.blogUse: Timer widget — no blog role

components.name: Sortable List
components.blogUse: Drag-sortable list — app pattern

components.name: 3D Carousel
components.blogUse: 3D rotating carousel — flashy and heavy

components.name: Feature Carousel
components.blogUse: Marketing feature carousel — off-pattern for a blog

components.name: Loading Carousel
components.blogUse: Loading-state carousel — app pattern

components.name: Pixel Heading (Char)
components.blogUse: Pixel-art heading — retro aesthetic clashes with Swiss minimal (could be a fun 404 headline if you loosen up)

components.name: Pixel Heading (Word)
components.blogUse: Pixel-art heading per word — same as above

components.name: Pixel Paragraph
components.blogUse: Pixelated paragraph — off-style

components.name: Pixel Paragraph Inv
components.blogUse: Inverted pixel paragraph — off-style

components.name: Text Gif
components.blogUse: GIF-filled text — too flashy for minimal design

notes: 1) Nearly every component is client-interactive ('use client' + motion) — in Astro each must be a React island (client:visible for below-fold like Code Block cards, client:load for header/nav); none of these render animations at SSR time, but they SSR their initial markup fine under @astrojs/react. 2) React 19: docs don't pin a React version; the kit is developed against current Next.js and `motion`, both React-19-compatible — but since components are copied source, any incompatibility is directly patchable. 3) Registry install assumes a shadcn-initialized project (components.json, cn util in lib/utils); run `npx shadcn@latest init` in the Astro project first, or copy sources manually and provide `cn` yourself. 4) Some components import sibling cult-ui components (e.g. Direction Aware Tabs demo uses BgAnimateButton) and shadcn primitives (Button, Dialog, Drawer for Intro Disclosure) — the CLI resolves these transitively. 5) Heavy deps to avoid on a minimal blog: shader heroes (Hero Dithering/Heatmap/Liquid Metal/Static Radial Gradient) require @paper-design/shaders-react (WebGL); Canvas Fractal Grid, Shader Lens Blur, Grid Beam, and Stripe Bg Guides are canvas/animation-loop based — if used as backgrounds, gate with prefers-reduced-motion and client:idle. 6) Slug gotcha: Expandable Card lives at /docs/components/expandable (r/expandable.json); /docs/components/expandable-card 404s. Slugs for non-deep-fetched components are inferred kebab-case from the sidebar names and a few (3D Carousel, Pixel Heading variants, LightBoard, MacOS Dock) may differ slightly — verify against the sidebar before scripting installs. 7) The /docs/components index itself 404s; the docs root (https://www.cult-ui.com/docs) carries the full sidebar. Homepage claims 78 free components; the sidebar enumerated ~78 across categories and this catalogue covers all of them — premium items (Cult Pro at pro.cult-ui.com: Apple device mockups, Analytics Chart, hero blocks, etc.) are paid and excluded. 8) Dark-first: components are dark-mode compatible (standard shadcn CSS-variable theming); your theme switcher itself isn't in this kit — build it with a Texture/Neumorph button inside the Toolbar Expandable.
